1.2: heart divided between Baal and the Lord.
1.17: . ― Ephraim pretends to worship the true God, while he worships idols, and that is how he became a real snare for the people.
1.10: For seven days, during the Feast of Tabernacles, the Jews lived in tents in remembrance of their fathers in the desert.
1.24: Jezrahel ; the most fertile valley of Palestine, is taken here for the whole country, and even for the whole nation of the Hebrews.
1.5: I met you in the desert from Sinai, at the time of the Exodus from Egypt.
1.23: . — This return, this conversion to the Lord and to the house of David, took place only very imperfectly after the captivity: thus, it is merely a foreshadowing of a return from a longer captivity and a more perfect conversion, when the Jews return to Jesus Christ, the true son and successor of David (see Romans ,
1.12: . An east wind will come: that is to say, the army of the Assyrians.
1.29: ); which led to a change in the name of Bethel , which means House of God , in that of Bethaven , which means Vanity House or’ idol . ― The Lord is alive ; oath formula which is equivalent to: I swear by the Lord .
1.19: . they will tell the mountains . Jesus used the same words in Luke ,
1.7: Chanaan ; That is to say, Israel corrupted like him. Often the prophets give things and people figurative names, which denote their good or bad qualities (see Ezekiel
1.16: ). Tyr . city described by Ezekiel Chapters

2.1: The number of the children of Israel will be like the sand of the sea, which cannot be measured or counted; and in the place where it was said to them, «You are not my people,» it will be said to them, «Children of the living God.»
2.2: And the children of Judah and the children of Israel shall gather together, they shall choose one leader for themselves and go up out of the land, for great shall be the day of Jezreel.
2.3: Say to your brothers: "Ammi" and to your sisters: "Ruchama"«
2.4: Plead against your mother, plead, for she is no longer my wife, and I am no longer her husband. Let her remove from her face her prostitution and adultery from the midst of her breasts,
2.5: lest I strip her naked and make her as on the day of her birth, and make her like the desert, and make her a parched land, and kill her with thirst.
2.6: I will have no compassion for her children, because they are children of prostitution.
2.7: For their mother played the harlot; she who conceived them did shameful things, and said, «I will go after my lovers, who give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, my oil and my drink.»
2.8: Therefore, I will block your path with thorns, I will build a wall, and she will no longer find her ways.
2.9: She will pursue her lovers but not catch them; she will seek them but not find them. Then she will say, "I will go back to my first husband, for I was happier then than now."«
2.10: She did not acknowledge that it was I who gave her the wheat, the new wine and the oil, who multiplied the silver and the gold for her, which they used for Baal.
2.11: That is why I will take back my wheat in its season and my new wine in its season, and I will take away my wool and my linen, which serve to cover her nakedness.
2.12: And now I will uncover her shame in the eyes of her lovers, and no one will release her from my grasp.
2.13: I will put an end to all her revelry, her festivals, her new moons, her Sabbaths, and all her solemnities.
2.14: I will devastate her vineyards and her fig trees, of which she said, "This is the wages my lovers gave me," and I will reduce them to forests, and wild beasts will devour them.
2.15: I will punish her for the days of the Baals, to whom she offered incense, when she adorned herself with her ring and her necklace and went after her lovers and me, she forgot me, oracle of the Lord.
2.16: Therefore, I will allure her and lead her into the desert, and I will speak tenderly to her,
2.17: and from there I will give her her vineyards and the valley of Achor as a door of hope, and there she will respond as in the days of her youth and as in the day when she went up out of the land of Egypt.
2.18: On that day, declares the Lord, you will call me: «My husband» and you will no longer call me: «My Baal».
2.19: I will remove the names of the Baals from his mouth, and they will no longer be mentioned by name.
2.20: I will make a covenant for them on that day with the wild beasts, with the birds of the air and the reptiles of the earth; I will remove the bow, the sword and war from the land, and I will make them lie down in safety.
2.21: I will betroth you to me forever, I will betroth you to me in righteousness and justice, in grace and tenderness,
2.22: I will betroth you to me in faithfulness, and you will know the Lord.
2.23: And it will happen on that day, I will answer, declares the Lord, I will answer the heavens and they will answer the earth,
2.24: The earth will respond to the wheat, the new wine, and the oil, and they will respond to Jezreel.
2.25: I will sow Israel for myself in the land and I will have mercy on Lo-Ruhamah; I will say to Lo-Ammi, «You are my people,» and he will say, «My God.»

3.1: And the Lord said to me, «Go again and love a woman who is loved by a lover and is an adulteress, just as the Lord loves the children of Israel, while they turn to other gods and love raisin cakes.»
3.2: I bought it for fifteen shekels of silver, a homer of barley and a lethec of barley,
3.3: And I said to him, «For many days you will stay here with me; you will not prostitute yourself and you will not belong to any other man, and I will do the same for you.»
3.4: For many days the children of Israel will remain without king and without leader, without sacrifice and without pillar, without ephod and without teraphim.
3.5: After this the children of Israel will convert and seek again the Lord, their God, and David, their king; they will return trembling to the Lord and to his goodness, at the end of days.

4.1: Listen to the word of the Lord, children of Israel, for the Lord has a case against the inhabitants of the land because there is neither truth, nor compassion, nor knowledge of God in the land.
4.2: They perjure themselves, they lie, they kill, they steal, they commit adultery, they do violence and spilled blood touches spilled blood.
4.3: That is why the land mourns and all who dwell in it are powerless, even the beasts of the field and the birds of the air, even the fish of the sea disappear.
4.4: But let no one dispute and let no one claim, for your people are like those who would have a lawsuit with the priest.
4.5: You will stumble during the day, the prophet will also stumble with you during the night, and I will kill your mother.
4.6: My people perish for lack of knowledge; because you have rejected knowledge, I will reject you from my priesthood. You have forgotten the law of your God; I also will forget your children.
4.7: While they still exist, they have sinned against me; I will turn their glory into shame.
4.8: They feed on the sins of my people; they desire only their iniquities.
4.9: The priest will be like the people; I will avenge his ways on him and bring his deeds back upon him.
4.10: They will eat but not be satisfied, they will prostitute themselves but not multiply, because they have abandoned the Lord and will no longer serve him.
4.11: Lust, wine, and new wine make you lose your head.
4.12: My people consult their wood and their staff tell them the future, for a spirit of prostitution has led them astray, and they have prostituted themselves away from their God.
4.13: They offer sacrifices on the mountaintops and burn incense on the hills, under the oak, the poplar, and the terebinth, because their shade is pleasant. Therefore, if your daughters become prostitutes and your young women commit adultery,
4.14: I will not punish your daughters for playing the harlot, nor your daughters-in-law for their adulteries, for they themselves go off with prostitutes and sacrifice with courtesans. And the foolish people are doomed.
4.15: If you commit adultery, O Israel, let not Judah be guilty. Do not come to Gilgal, nor go up to Beth-aven, nor swear an oath, saying, «As the Lord lives.»
4.16: Because Israel was stubborn like a stubborn heifer, now the Lord will feed them like lambs in an open plain.
4.17: Ephraim is attached to idols, leave him alone.
4.18: As soon as they finish drinking, they turn to prostitution; their leaders aspire only to ignominy.
4.19: The wind has enveloped them in its wings, and they will be ashamed because of their sacrifices.

5.1: Hear this, O priests, pay attention, house of Israel, house of the king, give ear, for the sentence is against you, because you have been a snare at Maspha and a net spread on Tabor.
5.2: By their sacrifices, they have reached the limit of their transgressions, but I will be a punishment for all of you.
5.3: I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hidden from me; yet you have prostituted yourself, Ephraim, Israel has defiled itself.
5.4: Their works do not permit them to return to their God, for a spirit of prostitution is in their midst and they do not know the Lord.
5.5: The pride of Israel testifies against him; Israel and Ephraim will fall because of their iniquity, and Judah will fall with them.
5.6: With their sheep and their cattle, they will go to look for the Lord, but they will not find him; he has separated himself from them.
5.7: They have deceived the Lord, for they have begotten foreign sons; now the next moon will devour them, along with their possessions.
5.8: Sound the trumpet at Gibeah, the trumpet at Ramah, raise the alarm at Bethaven. Take heed, Benjamin.
5.9: Ephraim will be devastated on the day of punishment; I declare a certain thing to the tribes of Israel.
5.10: The princes of Judah were like those who move boundary stones; I will pour out my wrath on them like water.
5.11: Ephraim is oppressed, broken by judgment, because he wanted to go after vile idols.
5.12: And I am like a moth to Ephraim, like rottenness to the house of Judah.
5.13: When Ephraim saw his illness and Judah his injury, Ephraim went to Assyria and sent word to a king to avenge him, but that king will not be able to heal you and your wound will not be taken away.
5.14: For I will be like a lion to Ephraim, like a young lion to the house of Judah; I, I will tear them apart and go away; I will carry off my prey, and no one will snatch it from me.
5.15: I will go away, I will return to my home, until they acknowledge their guilt and seek my face. In their distress, they will seek me.

6.1: Come and let us return to the Lord
6.2: For it is he who has torn us apart, he will heal us; he strikes, but he will bind up our wounds.
6.3: After two days he will revive us, on the third day he will raise us up, and we will live in his presence. Let us acknowledge the Lord; let us strive to acknowledge him. He will rise like the dawn, and he will come to us like a shower, like the late rains that water the earth.
6.4: What shall I do to you, Ephraim? What shall I do to you, Judah? Your piety is like a morning cloud, like the passing morning dew.
6.5: That is why I cut them to pieces by the prophets, I killed them by the words of my mouth; your judgment is the light that will dawn.
6.6: For I desire godliness, not sacrifice; the knowledge of God, rather than burnt offerings.
6.7: But, like Adam, they have transgressed the covenant; there, they have been unfaithful to me.
6.8: Gilead is a city of evildoers, marked with traces of blood.
6.9: Like bandits lying in ambush, so a band of priests murders on the road to Shechem, for they commit wickedness.
6.10: In the house of Israel I have seen horrible things; there Ephraim has committed adultery, there Israel has defiled itself.
6.11: You too, Judah, a harvest is destined for you, when I bring back my people from captivity.

7.1: When I wanted to heal Israel, then the iniquity of Ephraim and the wickedness of Samaria were revealed, for they practice falsehood, the thief enters the house, the robber spreads outside.
7.2: And they do not say in their hearts that I remember all their wickedness. Now their crimes surround them, they are there before me.
7.3: They amuse the king with their wickedness and the princes with their lies.
7.4: They are all adulterers, like an oven lit by the baker, who stops stirring the fire from the time he has kneaded the dough until it has risen.
7.5: On the day of our king, the princes made themselves sick from the heat of the wine; he stretched out his hand with the mockers.
7.6: For in their plot, they put their hearts as in an oven. Their baker slept all night, and in the morning he was ablaze like a burning flame.
7.7: They are all heated like an oven and they devour their judges. All their kings have fallen, without any of them crying out to me.
7.8: Ephraim mingles with the peoples, Ephraim is a cake that has not been turned over.
7.9: Strangers have devoured his strength and he doesn't know it, white hairs dot his head and he doesn't know it.
7.10: Ephraim's pride testifies against him; they do not return to the Lord their God, nor do they seek him, despite all this.
7.11: Ephraim has become like a dove, simple and without understanding; they call upon Egypt, they go to Assyria.
7.12: While they go there, I will spread my net over them, I will make them fall like the birds of the air, I will punish them as was announced in their assembly.
7.13: Woe to them, for they have fled from me. Ruin upon them, for they have been faithless to me. And I would like to save them, but they speak lies against me.
7.14: They do not cry out to me in their hearts, when they wail on their beds. They fret about wheat and new wine, turning away from me.
7.15: I instructed them and strengthened their arms, yet they plot evil against me.
7.16: They turn, but not upwards; they are like a deceptive bow; their leaders will fall by the sword because of the fury of their tongues, and they will be laughed at in the land of Egypt.

8.1: Sound the trumpet! Like an eagle, disaster has descended upon the house of the Lord because they have transgressed my covenant and sinned against my law.
8.2: They cry out to me, «My God, we Israel have known you!»
8.3: Israel has become disgusted with good; let the enemy pursue it.
8.4: They made kings, but not from me; they appointed leaders, but whom I did not know; they made idols out of their silver and gold, so that the silver and gold might be taken from them.
8.5: Your calf has disgusted me, Samaria; my anger has burned against them. How long will they not be purified?
8.6: For he too comes from Israel, made by a craftsman, and is not a God, for he will be cut to pieces, the calf of Samaria.
8.7: Because they have sown the wind, they will reap the whirlwind. There will be no ear of grain for him, but wheat that will not yield flour, and if it does yield flour, foreigners will devour it.
8.8: Israel is devoured; now they have become among the nations like a worthless object.
8.9: For they themselves have gone up to Assyria, the wild ass lives apart, Ephraim has bought himself lovers.
8.10: Even if they give gifts to the nations, I will gather them against them and they will tremble for a little while under the burden of the king of princes.
8.11: Ephraim multiplied altars in sin, and these altars caused him to fall into sin.
8.12: Even if I write a thousand articles of my law for him, they are regarded as something foreign.
8.13: The sacrifices they offer me are meat they slaughter and eat; the Lord does not accept them. Now he will remember their iniquity and punish their sins; they will return to Egypt.
8.14: Israel has forgotten its Maker and built palaces, and Judah has multiplied its fortified cities. I will send fire upon its cities, and it will devour its fortresses.

9.1: Do not rejoice, Israel, nor be glad like the nations, for you have prostituted yourself away from your God; you have loved the wages of a prostitute on all the threshing floors.
9.2: Neither the drying area nor the vat will nourish them, and the new wine will reject them.
9.3: They will not dwell in the land of the Lord; Ephraim will return to Egypt, and they will eat unclean food in Assyria.
9.4: They will not pour out wine offerings to the Lord, nor will their sacrifices be pleasing to him; it will be like the bread of mourning to them; those who eat of it will become unclean, for their bread will be for themselves; it will not enter the house of the Lord.
9.5: What will you do on the solemn day, on the Lord's feast day?
9.6: For behold, they have departed before devastation; Egypt will gather them up. Memphis will bury them, their precious silver objects will be seized by the thistle, and thorns will invade their tents.
9.7: The days of punishment have come, the days of retribution have come. Israel will know it. The prophet is mad, the man of the spirit is delirious. Because of the greatness of your iniquity and your great hostility.
9.8: The watchman of Ephraim is with my God; the prophet finds a fowler's snare in all his ways, persecution in the house of his God.
9.9: They are corrupt to the core, as in the days of Gibeah; the Lord will remember their iniquity, he will punish their sins.
9.10: Like grapes in the desert, I found Israel; like early ripening figs on a young fig tree, I saw your ancestors. But they came to Beelphegor, they devoted themselves to the shameful idol and became as abominable as the object of their love.
9.11: The glory of Ephraim will fly away like a bird; there will be no more childbirth, no more pregnancy, no more conception.
9.12: Even if they were to raise their children, I would deprive them of them before they became men, for woe to them also, if I withdraw from them.
9.13: Ephraim, when I look towards Tyre, is planted in a beautiful meadow, and Ephraim is going to lead his children to the slaughterer.
9.14: Give them, Lord, what will you give them? Give them barren wombs and withered breasts.
9.15: All their wickedness is in Galgal, for it is there that I have come to hate them. Because of the evil of their deeds, I will drive them out of my house; I will no longer have love for them: all their leaders are rebels.
9.16: Ephraim is stricken, his root is withered, they will bear no fruit. And if they even conceive, I will destroy the cherished fruits of their womb.
9.17: My God will reject them, because they did not listen to him, and they will wander among the nations.

10.1: Israel is a luxuriant vine that has borne fruit. The more abundant its fruit, the more altars it built; the more beautiful the land became, the more beautiful the pillars they made.
10.2: Their hearts are hypocritical: they will bear the consequences. He will overturn their altars and destroy their pillars.
10.3: Soon they will say, "We no longer have a king, because we did not fear the Lord, and what will the king do for us?"«
10.4: They will speak words, make empty oaths, conclude alliances, and judgment will spring up like poppies in the furrows of the fields.
10.5: For the heifers of Bethaven, the inhabitants of Samaria are afraid, for his people mourn over the idol and his priests tremble over him, for his glory which has emigrated far from him.
10.6: She too will be transported to Assyria, as an offering to the avenging king; confusion will seize Ephraim, and Israel will be ashamed of its plans.
10.7: Samaria is destroyed, its king is like a straw on the surface of the water.
10.8: The high places of Aven, the sin of Israel, will be destroyed; thorns and briers will climb their altars. They will say to the mountains, "Cover us," and to the hills, "Fall on us.".
10.9: Since the days of Gibeah you have sinned, Israel; they persist in their wickedness. Will not war, declared against the sons of iniquity, reach them at Gibeah?
10.10: I will punish them as I see fit, and the peoples will be gathered against them, when they are bound for their two sins.
10.11: Ephraim was a well-trained heifer, who delighted in treading the wheat. And I put the yoke on his fine neck; I will harness Ephraim, Judah will plow, Jacob will harrow.
10.12: Sow your seeds in righteousness, reap in mercy, break up your unplowed lands, it is time to seek the Lord, until he comes to shower righteousness on you.
10.13: But you have plowed wickedness, you have reaped iniquity, you have eaten the fruit of lies. You have trusted in your own ways, in the great number of your mighty men.
10.14: The tumult arises among your people and all your fortresses will be devastated, as Salman devastated Beth-Arbel, on the day of the war when the mother was crushed over her children.
10.15: This is what Bethel has done to you because of your extreme wickedness. The dawn will break, and the king of Israel will be no more.

11.1: When Israel was a child, I loved him, and from Egypt, I sent appeals to my son.
11.2: They were called upon and they turned away. They offered sacrifices to Baal and incense to idols.
11.3: And I was teaching Ephraim to walk, I would take them by the arms and they didn't understand that I was taking care of them.
11.4: I led them with humanity, with bonds of love, I was to them like the one who would have lifted the yoke from their jaws and I bent down to him and made him eat.
11.5: He will not return to the land of Egypt, and Assyria will be its king, because they refused to convert.
11.6: The sword will be brandished in its cities, it will break down the bars and devour, because of their designs.
11.7: My people are determined to separate from me; they are called to the heavens, but none of them raise their eyes.
11.8: How could I forsake you, Ephraim, or hand you over, Israel? How could I let you become like Adamah, or make you like Sheboim? My heart turns within me, and all my compassions are stirred.
11.9: I will not give vent to my fierce anger, I will not destroy Ephraim again. For I am God, and not a man: the Holy One is in your midst, and I will not come in my wrath.
11.10: They will go after the Lord, like a lion, when he roars. When he roars, his sons will come trembling from the West.
11.11: They will come running, trembling like a bird, from Egypt and like a dove, from the land of Assyria, and I will make them dwell in their houses, declares the Lord.

12.1: Ephraim has surrounded me with lies, and the house of Israel with deceit; Judah also is unrestrained toward God and toward the Holy One, who is faithful.
12.2: Ephraim feeds on the wind and chases after the east wind. All day long he piles up lies and violence; they make a pact with Assyria, and oil is transported to Egypt.
12.3: The Lord also has a case against Judah; he will punish Jacob according to his ways, he will repay him according to his deeds.
12.4: In his mother's womb, Jacob surpassed his brother, and in his strength he wrestled with God.
12.5: He wrestled with the angel and prevailed, he wept and begged for mercy, he found him later at Bethel and there God spoke with us.
12.6: But the Lord is the God of hosts, his name is the Lord.
12.7: And you will return to your God, maintain goodness and justice, and always hope in your God.
12.8: Chanaan holds a rigged scale in his hand; he likes to extort.
12.9: Ephraim said, «Yet I have become rich, I have acquired wealth; in all my gains, no wrongdoing will be found against me that constitutes a sin.»
12.10: And I am the Lord your God, from the land of Egypt, I will again make you dwell in tents, as on the days of the festival.
12.11: And I spoke to the prophets, and I multiplied the vision, and through the prophets I spoke in parables.
12.12: If Gilead is vanity, they will be nothing; in Gilgal they sacrificed bulls, so their altars will be like heaps of stones on the furrows of the fields.
12.13: Jacob fled to the plain of Aram, Israel served as a wife and as a wife he tended flocks.
12.14: And through a prophet, the Lord brought Israel up out of Egypt, and through a prophet they were protected.
12.15: Ephraim has bitterly provoked divine anger; He will shed his blood upon himself, and his Lord will repay him for his outrage.

13.1: Whenever Ephraim spoke, people trembled; he rose up in Israel. But he became guilty through Baal and died.
13.2: And now they continue to sin; with their money they have made a cast statue, idols according to their own idea: the work of craftsmen, all of it. It is said of them: «Prisoners of men, they kiss calves.»
13.3: That is why they will be like a morning cloud and like the morning dew that dissipates, like chaff that the wind blows from the threshing floor and like smoke that goes away through a window.
13.4: And I am the Lord your God, from the land of Egypt; you shall know no other God but me, and besides me there is no Savior.
13.5: I knew you in the desert, in the land of drought.
13.6: When they had their fill of food, they were satisfied, they were satisfied, and their hearts were lifted up, and because of this they forgot me.
13.7: I will be like a lion to them, like a panther, I will lie in wait for them at the edge of the road.
13.8: I will pounce on them like a bear deprived of her cubs, I will tear open the covering of their hearts and devour them there like a lioness, the beast of the field will tear them to pieces.
13.9: What destroys you, Israel, is that you are against me, against the one who is your help.
13.10: Where is your king, so that he may save you in all your cities? And where are your judges of whom you said, «Give me a king and princes?»
13.11: I will give you a king in my anger, but I will take him from you in my fury.
13.12: Ephraim's iniquity is bound, his sin is set aside.
13.13: The pains of childbirth come to him; he is a child devoid of wisdom, for when the time comes, he does not present himself to be born.
13.14: I will deliver them from the power of Sheol, I will redeem them from death. Where is your plague, O death? Where is your destruction, O Sheol? Repentance is hidden from my eyes.
13.15: For Ephraim will flourish among his brothers, but the east wind will come, the breath of the Lord will rise from the desert, his spring will dry up, his fountain will run dry, he will plunder the treasures of all precious things.

14.1: Samaria will be punished because she rebelled against her God; they will fall by the sword. Their little children will be crushed, and their pregnant women will be ripped open.
14.2: Return, Israel, to the Lord, for you have fallen because of your iniquity.
14.3: Take words with you and return to the Lord, and say to him: «Take away all iniquity and take what is good. Let us offer you, instead of bulls, the words of our lips.”.
14.4: Assur will not save us, we will not ride horses, and we will no longer say, "Our God," to the work of our hands. O you in whom the orphan finds compassion.
14.5: I will heal their unfaithfulness, I will love them wholeheartedly, for my anger has departed from them.
14.6: I will be like the dew to Israel; he will flourish like the lily, he will put down roots like Lebanon.
14.7: Its offspring will spread, its glory will be like that of the olive tree, and its fragrance like that of Lebanon.
14.8: Those who come to rest in its shade will revive the wheat; they will grow like the vine, and its name will be like the wine of Lebanon.
14.9: Ephraim, what more could he do with idols? It is I who answer him, who look at him; I am like a green cypress tree, from me comes your fruit.
14.10: Let the wise understand these things, let the intelligent recognize them, for the ways of the Lord are right; the righteous will walk in them, but the rebellious will fall in them.
